title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary & olive oil. This easy recipe brings the taste of Italy to your kitchen. Perfect for dipping or sandwiches!

authentic Italian Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients for Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil
Pro Tips for the Best Focaccia Genovese
seo.imageAlt: A golden-brown Focaccia Genovese with fresh rosemary sprigs and a drizzle of olive oil, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the irresistible aroma and taste of homemade Focaccia Genovese, a classic Italian bread perfect for any meal.

description: Focaccia Genovese is a classic Italian flatbread from Liguria, known for its airy texture, olive oil richness, and characteristic dimples. This recipe elevates the traditional version with fragrant rosemary, offering an aromatic and utterly delicious baking experience. Perfect as an appetizer, side dish, or base for sandwiches, it brings a taste of Genoa straight to your table.

ingredients.notes: plus more for oiling and drizzling

instructions.title: Activate Yeast and Mix Dry Ingredients
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the bread flour and fine sea salt.

instructions.title: Form the Dough
instructions.text: Pour the activated yeast mixture and 30ml of the olive oil into the dry ingredients. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Alternatively, use a stand mixer with a dough hook.

instructions.title: First Rise (Bulk Fermentation)
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size. This slow rise develops flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare for Second Rise
instructions.text: Generously oil a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan with olive oil. Gently transfer the risen dough into the prepared pan. Gently stretch the dough with your fingertips to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it completely.

instructions.title: Dimple and Second Rise
instructions.text: Using your fingertips, create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough. Drizzle with the remaining 30ml of olive oil. Sprinkle evenly with the chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. Cover loosely and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220°C (425°F). Bake the focaccia for 20-25 minutes, or until it is golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be lightly browned and crisp.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the focaccia from the oven. For an extra touch of flavor and moisture, immediately drizzle with a final splash of extra virgin olive oil. Let it cool on a wire rack for a few minutes before slicing and serving warm. Buon appetito!

tips: For the signature airy texture, use strong bread flour and ensure your yeast is active. Do not rush the proofing process.
A generous amount of good quality extra virgin olive oil is crucial for authentic flavor and moisture. Do not skimp on it!
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh on the day it is baked. If you have leftovers, refresh them briefly in a warm oven or toaster.
article: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a beloved Italian Baking dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
